title: www_justice_gov_uk_courts_procedure_rules_civil_contents_frontmatter_Notes30_a_1_06b5f25b0a.html
content: NOTES TO ACCOMPANY FEBRUARY 2003 30th SUPPLEMENT – Civil Procedure Rules – Justice UK Cookie Settings Are you OK with cookies? We use small files called ‘cookies’ on www.justice.gov.uk. Some are essential to make the site work, some help us to understand how we can improve your experience, and some are set by third parties. You can choose to turn off the non-essential cookies. Which cookies are you happy for us to use? I am OK with cookies Only use essential cookies Choose which cookies we use Close Choose which cookies we use Marketing cookies Off On Google Analytics Off On We use Google Analytics to measure how you use the website so we can improve it based on user needs. We do not allow Google Analytics to use or share the data about how you use this site. See our Google Analytics cookies Google Analytics Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years _gid These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 24 hours _gat These help us to manage how we collect analytics when we have lots of visitors on the site at one time 10 minutes Google Analytics Events Off On We use Google Analytics events to measure clicks within the pages and extended usage of the site See our Google Analytics Events cookies Google Analytics Events Name Purpose Expires _ga These help us count how many people visit justice.gov.uk by tracking if you’ve visited before 2 years Essential cookies Always on All users These cookies will always need to be on because they make our site work. See our All users cookies All users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_test_cookie This is used to test whether the browser accepts cookies When you close your browser PHPSESSID This is used to link your device to the information sent to the server from your browser. It is typically used to avoid you having to retype information when moving from one page to another. When you close your browser ccfw-banner-hidden Lets us know you have chosen which cookies are used so we can stop the cookie banner appearing when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-gtm-allowed Lets us know you have accepted certain cookies so we can stop them loading when you return to the site. 1 year ccfw-time This is used to store the cookie expiry dates so we know when to ask you again for consent. 1 year ccfw_gtm_id This cookie is used to link the cookie consent banner to Google Tag Manager 1 year Logged in users These cookies apply when users log in. They will always be on because they make our site work. See our Logged in users cookies Logged in users Name Purpose Expires wordpress_[hash] This authenticates you when you log in to the admin area When you close your browser wordpress_logged_in This shows the site that you’re logged in and who you are so you can access the functions you need When you close your browser wordpress_sec If you are logged in as a site admin, this stores your authentication details. When you close your browser wp-settings-{time}-[UID] The number on the end [UID] is your individual user ID from the users database. 1 year > Save cookie preferences . Home   Courts Procedure rules Offenders Search Courts Procedure rules Civil contents frontmatter NOTES TO ACCOMPANY FEBRUARY 2003 30th SUPPLEMENT Home » Courts » Procedure rules » Civil » contents » frontmatter » NOTES TO ACCOMPANY FEBRUARY 2003 30th SUPPLEMENT Menu ≡ NOTES TO ACCOMPANY FEBRUARY 2003 30th SUPPLEMENT Introduction The 30 th update of the Civil Procedure Rules introduces Part 63 which governs the procedure for claims for intellectual property rights, in particular patents, registered designs and registered trade marks. This replaces the provisions in Practice Direction 49E from 1 April 2003. The update also contains a number of PD changes. The most notable changes are: a replacement Practice Direction 5B on the pilot scheme for communication and filing of documents by e-mail coming into force on 1 April; amendments to Practice Direction 37 providing for the payment of money into court to be sent to the Court Funds Office coming into force on 1 April; changes to Practice Direction 52 to take account of changes in appeal procedures for health care professionals coming into force on 1 April; a new Practice Direction 63 to supplement the above new part coming into force on 1 April; a new Practice Direction on Restraint Orders and appointment of receivers in connection with criminal proceedings and investigations to supplement RSC Order 115 coming into force on 17 February; a new Practice Direction on anti-social behaviour which makes provision for an application in existing proceedings in a county court for an order (including an interim order) under the Crime and Disorder Act 1998 by a relevant authority coming into force on 1 April; a new Practice Direction containing general provisions about proceedings in the High Court under Parts 5 and 8 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 which will come into force on the commencement of Chapter 2 of Part 5 and Chapter 2 of Part 8 of the Act; amendments to the practice direction on protocols to include a number of additional provisions designed to encourage parties to act reasonably in exchanging information and documents at an early stage and generally to seek to settle their dispute without recourse to litigation. Amendments to existing material are included as follows: Rules Part 25 Rule 25.13(2) Existing sub-paragraph (a) replaced by new text. Sub-paragraph (b) omitted. Part 36 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 36.6 Existing paragraph (3) replaced by new text. Paragraph (4) omitted. Part 37 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Rule 37.1 Existing paragraph (1) replaced by new text. Part 49 Rule 49.2 Sub-paragraph (d) revoked. Part 56 Rule 56.4 ‘and’ omitted at the end of sub-paragraph (c). New sub-paragraph (e) inserted. Part 63 THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK New part inserted on patents and intellectual property claims. These replace Practice Direction 49E. Practice Directions Practice Direction 5B Existing PD replaced by new PD. Practice Direction 28 In paragraph 2.1(2) ‘listing questionnaire’ replaced by ‘pre-trial check lists’. Practice Direction 29 In paragraph 2.4 ‘listing questionnaire’ replaced by ‘pre-trial check lists’. Practice Direction 31 In paragraph 8 the reference to ‘paragraph 27’ replaced by ‘paragraphs 28.1–28.3’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 36 Existing paragraph 4.1 replaced by new wording. New paragraph 4.2 inserted. Existing paragraphs 8.1 & 8.3 replaced by new wording. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 37 Existing paragraphs 1.1 & 1.2 replaced by new wording. Existing paragraph 2.1 replaced by new paragraphs 2.1 and 2.1A. New paragraphs 11.1–11.5 inserted. Practice Direction 43–48 In paragraph 3.3 ‘13.6’ replaced by ‘13.5’. Practice Direction 49E Revoked (replaced by provisions in Part 63 and accompanying PD). Practice Direction 52 New entries inserted in the table in paragraph 20. Title and text of paragraph 22.3 amended. Practice Direction 55 New paragraphs 1.7 and 1.8 inserted. Practice Direction 58 New paragraph 4.4 inserted. Practice Direction 60 New paragraph 5.3 inserted. Practice Direction 62 Existing Paragraph 2.3 replaced by new wording. New paragraphs 12.4–12.6 inserted. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Practice Direction 63 New Practice Direction on ‘Patents and Other Intellectual Property Claims’ inserted to supplement new Part 63 rules. Practice Direction 75 New paragraphs 1.1(4), 1.2(5), 1.2(6) inserted. Existing paragraphs 1.3(2)(b) and (c) replaced by new paragraphs 1.3(2)(b), (c) and (d). Existing paragraphs 1.3(3)(c) and (d) replaced by 1.3(3)(c) to (f). Existing paragraphs 3.1 and 4.1 replaced by new text. Existing paragraphs 5.1(2) and (3) replaced by new paragraphs 5.1(2) to 5.1(4). Other Practice Directions Schedule 1 RSC Order 115 Practice Direction New Practice Direction inserted on the subject of Restraint orders and the appointment of receivers in connection with criminal proceedings and investigations. Directors Disqualification Paragraphs 11.4 & 33.9 amended by replacing ‘listing questionnaires’ with ‘pre-trial check lists (listing questionnaire)’. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Anti-social behaviour New Practice Direction inserted on bringing applications in a county court for an order under section 1B(4) of the Crime and Disorder Act 1998. Civil Recovery New Practice Direction inserted containing general provisions about proceedings in the High Court under Parts 5 and 8 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002. THIS LINK HAS BEEN ARCHIVED. PLEASE CHECK THIS LINK Protocols Practice Direction New paragraphs 3.3–3.5 inserted. Paragraph 4 re-numbered as 4.1. New paragraphs 4.2–4.10 inserted. Judicial Review Pre-Action Protocol Judicial Review Management Unit address amended. Forms New forms inserted as follows: N242 Notice of payment into court (under order – Part 37) N243B Notice of instruction to Court Funds Office The following forms are issued to replace existing forms: Ex140 Record of Examination (individual) Ex141 Record of Examination (officer, etc) N211C Notes for Defendant (CPR Part 20) N242A Notice of payment into court (in settlement – Part 36) N243A/F201 Notice of acceptance and request for payment (Part 36) N292 Order on settlement on behalf of child or patient Coming into force All changes come into force on 1 April 2003 with the exception of the following: The new Practice Direction on Restraint Orders and Appointment of Receivers in Connection with Criminal Proceedings and Investigations will come into effect on 17 February 2003. The amendments to the Part 75 Practice Direction will come into effect on 17 February 2003. The Practice Direction on the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 Parts 5 and 8: Civil Recovery will come into effect on the commencement of Chapter 2 of Part 5 and Chapter 2 of Part 8 of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002.
